channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: A daily outlier hunt across the AI industry. What is new in the past day, week, month, and quarter, ranked by relative rotation. A personal instrument for staying organized and courageous on the frontier.

item.title: Mellum2 Goes Open Source: A Fast Model for AI Workflows
item.description: microflash: Mellum2, a fast model for AI workflows, has been released as open source. The model is designed to enable faster inference and processing in AI applications.

item.title: Anthropic is about to IPO at a TRILLION DOLLARS
item.description: Wes Roth: Wes Roth claims Anthropic is about to go public at a trillion dollar valuation. The video covers news on LLMs, generative AI, and developments across major AI companies including OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, and open source projects.

item.title: GPT-5.6 about to DROP
item.description: Wes Roth: Wes Roth's YouTube channel covers AI industry news and developments. The video title suggests GPT-5.6 is imminent, framing speculation about OpenAI's product roadmap for an audience interested in LLMs, generative AI, and AGI timelines.

item.title: Pasted File Editor
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created Pasted File Editor, a web tool that automatically converts large text pastes into file attachments, mimicking Claude.ai's behavior. The tool supports direct file opening, image thumbnails, and drag-and-drop file uploads.

item.title: Pasted File Editor
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created Pasted File Editor, a web tool inspired by Claude's ability to auto-convert large text pastes into file attachments. The tool supports direct file opening, image thumbnails, and drag-and-drop file uploads into a textarea interface, built with Claude using Codex desktop.

item.title: [AINews] NVIDIA Cosmos 3, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and RTX Spark
item.description: Latent Space: NVIDIA announced Cosmos 3, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and RTX Spark. These represent significant product launches from NVIDIA that Jensen Huang is celebrating as a major achievement.

item.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system
item.description: spenvo: GitHub Copilot has introduced a new usage-based pricing model that prompted user reactions and discussion. The shift represents a change in how Copilot users are charged for the service.

item.title: Colorado Rolls Back Landmark AI Governance Law
item.description: mooreds: Colorado reversed course on a landmark AI governance law. The rollback reflects ongoing policy tensions around AI regulation and compliance frameworks.

item.title: Can the stockmarket swallow Anthropic, SpaceX and OpenAI?
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: Raises the question of whether the stock market has sufficient capacity to accommodate public offerings or major valuations of Anthropic, SpaceX, and OpenAI. This is a commentary on market dynamics and corporate financing prospects for leading AI companies.

item.title: After Automation: AI progress creates more work for humans, not less
item.description: ParkRanger: The post argues that AI progress is generating more work for humans rather than reducing it, contrary to common automation narratives. This reflects a perspective on AI's labor market and economic consequences.

item.title: Open source project contains hidden instruction for "AI" agents: delete my code
item.description: mbreese: A developer discovered hidden instructions embedded in an open-source project that were designed to trigger destructive behavior in AI agents. The incident highlights emerging security risks posed by autonomous AI systems interacting with untrusted code repositories and raises concerns about agent safety and manipulation.

item.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
item.description: typpo: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available through AWS integration. This makes OpenAI's latest models and code generation tool accessible via Amazon's cloud platform for broader enterprise adoption.

item.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
item.description: Simon Willison: Meta integrated an AI support chatbot with the ability to complete account recovery processes without sufficient safeguards. Hackers successfully exploited this by directly requesting the bot to link target Instagram accounts to attacker email addresses, demonstrating a critical security failure.

item.title: Alphabet announces $80B equity capital raise to expand AI infra and compute
item.description: gregschlom: Alphabet announced an $80 billion equity capital raise to fund expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure and compute capacity. The move reflects Alphabet's commitment to scaling AI compute resources and infrastructure investments.

item.title: Show HN: Mqtt Broker for 10 Years
item.description: skittleson: An MQTT broker designed for 10-year deployment lifespans on low-cost embedded hardware ($10-30), optimized for flash wear management and minimal external dependencies. The project prioritizes reliability and autonomy with no telemetry or phone-home updates, currently in beta after one month of testing.

item.title: Remote work, not AI, has sidelined recent college graduates, research finds
item.description: hi41: Research indicates that remote work policies, rather than AI adoption, are the primary factor limiting recent college graduate employment prospects. The finding challenges the common assumption that AI automation is displacing entry-level workers.

item.title: Show HN: Textile – A desktop app for weaving together bits of text
item.description: stack_framer: Textile is a macOS desktop app built with Electron that combines text fragments from multiple sources like clipboard, commands, and hardcoded strings. Users can build and modify dynamic strings step by step, save workflows, and execute them on demand via button click or keyboard shortcut.

item.title: Florida AG files lawsuit against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for deceptive practices
item.description: benwen: Florida's Attorney General filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman alleging deceptive practices. The action represents a regulatory challenge to the company's business conduct.

item.title: Thinking in Blender: Staged Executable Inverse Graphics with Vision-Language Models
item.description: Guangzhao He, Rundong Luo, Wei-Chiu Ma: Staged Executable Inverse Graphics (SEIG) is an agentic framework that reconstructs 3D scenes from single images by progressively refining geometry, materials, composition, and lighting directly as executable Blender code. The approach uses pretrained vision-language models without specialized foundation models, differentiable rendering, or multi-view supervision, with staged reconstruction improving reconstruction fidelity across pixel, perceptual, and semantic metrics.

item.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
item.description: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang: The paper identifies Perceptual Judgment Bias in multimodal LLMs used as judges, where models favor plausible text over visual correctness. The authors propose a solution using perceptual perturbation to create counterfactual examples and a unified training framework combining GRPO-based rewards with batch-ranking objectives to improve perceptual fidelity and evaluation consistency.

item.title: RoboDream: Compositional World Models for Scalable Robot Data Synthesis
item.description: Junjie Ye, Rong Xue, Basile Van Hoorick: RoboDream is an embodiment-centric world model that generates photorealistic robot demonstrations with novel objects and scenes by anchoring generation to rendered robot motion and conditioning on explicit scene and object priors. The approach enables two scaling capabilities: repurposing existing trajectories into new contexts without additional motion data, and prop-free teleoperation where operators manipulate empty air and the model synthesizes target objects and scenes. Real-world experimen

item.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
item.description: Yu-Cheng Shi, Zhen-Hao Xie, Jun-Tao Tang: ProtoAda is a framework for multimodal continual instruction tuning that uses format-aware task prototypes to improve task routing and reduce inter-task interference in sparse architectures. It consolidates updates in a geometry-aware manner to prevent gradient interference when learning tasks with distinct response structures sequentially.

item.title: From Zero to Hero: Training-Free Custom Concept Spawning in World Models
item.description: Kiymet Akdemir, Pinar Yanardag: SPAWN is a training-free technique for injecting user-specified visual concepts into autoregressive world models by swapping the pinned anchor in the first context memory slot with an external concept latent over a short injection window. The method enables controllable scene composition in interactive video generation by exploiting structural properties of image-to-video backbones, supporting concepts ranging from fine-grained entities to large-scale elements with consistent lighting and tempor

item.title: HumanNOVA: Photorealistic, Universal and Rapid 3D Human Avatar Modeling from a Single Image
item.description: Hezhen Hu, Wangbo Zhao, Lanqing Guo: HumanNOVA generates photorealistic 3D human avatars from a single RGB image in under one second. The approach uses a scalable data pipeline with 100k rigged assets and multi-camera captures, combined with a feed-forward, token-conditioned architecture based on triplanes.

item.title: VISReg: Variance-Invariance-Sketching Regularization for JEPA training
item.description: Haiyu Wu, Randall Balestriero, Morgan Levine: VISReg combines variance and Sliced-Wasserstein-based sketching objectives to prevent embedding collapse in JEPA self-supervised learning. It decouples scale and shape control while maintaining linear scalability and achieving state-of-the-art out-of-distribution performance.

item.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
item.description: Haowen Hou, Zhen Huang, Zheming Liang: AdaCodec is a predictive visual coding approach for video multimodal LLMs that adaptively encodes full reference frames when conditional predictive cost is high, and otherwise transmits compact P-tokens representing inter-frame changes like motion and residuals. It reduces visual token redundancy by exploiting temporal coherence in video, achieving better performance than per-frame RGB baselines while using up to 7x fewer tokens.

item.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
item.description: Yuxing Lu, Yushuhong Lin, Wenqi Shi: ClinEnv is an interactive benchmark for evaluating large language models as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions. It constructs clinical cases into ordered decision stages where models query specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses, with evaluation on both outcomes and information-gathering process.

item.title: Qwen3.7-Plus: Multimodal Agent Intelligence
item.description: meetpateltech: Qwen3.7-Plus is a multimodal model focused on agent intelligence capabilities. It processes multiple modalities and supports agentic reasoning and tasks.

item.title: Policy-based Foveated Imaging and Perception
item.description: Howard Xiao, Jan Ackermann, Boyang Deng: This work introduces a policy-based foveated imaging system that uses reinforcement learning to dynamically allocate limited pixel bandwidth to task-relevant regions during image acquisition. The approach leverages dual-stream sensor architectures to maintain low-resolution global context while focusing high-resolution capture on regions of interest, demonstrating significant performance gains under strict bandwidth constraints on real 200-megapixel dual-stream sensors.

item.title: VLMs are Good Teachers for Video Reasoning via Adaptive Test-Time Optimization
item.description: Junhao Cheng, Liang Hou, Tianxiong Zhong: This work shifts Vision-Language Models from problem solvers to teachers that extract task-specific rules and formulate differentiable rewards. A VGM Reasoner is guided via test-time online optimization of a lightweight LoRA module, achieving 16.7-point performance gains on video reasoning benchmarks over baseline VLM-as-Solver approaches.

item.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
item.description: Farhin Farhad Riya, Olivera Kotevska, Jinyuan Stella Sun: IntraShuffler is a middleware defense framework for Heterogeneous Differential Privacy Federated Learning that mitigates privacy inference attacks through parameter-level shuffling within privacy-compatible client buckets. It preserves epsilon-aware aggregation while reducing gradient recoverability by over 60% and inference accuracy from 0.78 to 0.33 across multiple datasets.

item.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
item.description: Haimin Hu: The paper presents a verifiable approach to certify high-probability safety for belief-space neural safety filters in interactive robotics using conformal prediction. It accounts for runtime inference reliability and reduces conservativeness in safety filtering while maintaining formal guarantees.

item.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
item.description: Elia Cunegatti, Marcus Vukojevic, Erik Nielsen: SubFit compresses LLMs by selecting Attention and FeedForward submodules non-contiguously and fitting lightweight residual bypasses to each, moving beyond full-layer granularity. The method operates post-training with only calibration data and achieves better perplexity-accuracy trade-offs than layer-level baselines across multiple sparsity levels and model scales.

item.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
item.description: Anshun Asher Zheng, Kanishka Misra, David I. Beaver: HERO'S JOURNEY is a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations. It covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families with controllable lexical grounding and identifiability conditions. Evaluation of state-of-the-art LLMs reveals limited and uneven rule induction abilities, with execution bottlenecks and gaps in procedural induction.

item.title: LongLive-RAG: A General Retrieval-Augmented Framework for Long Video Generation
item.description: Qixin Hu, Shuai Yang, Wei Huang: LongLive-RAG addresses error accumulation in long autoregressive video generation by treating previously generated latents as a searchable retrieval memory instead of relying solely on sliding-window attention. The method uses query embeddings to retrieve relevant historical context and introduces Window Temporal Delta Loss to improve discriminative retrieval.

item.title: Modeling Depth Ambiguity: A Mixture-Density Representation for Flying-Point-Free Depth Estimation
item.description: Siyuan Bian, Congrong Xu, Jun Gao: MDA is a mixture-density representation that predicts multiple depth hypotheses and their probabilities per pixel to eliminate flying-point artifacts at object boundaries. The approach extends to transparent objects and sky regions without significant runtime overhead and shows substantial improvements in boundary reconstruction across different backbones.

item.title: AFUN: Towards an Affordance Foundation Model for Functionality Understanding
item.description: Zhaoning Wang, Yi Zhong, Jiawei Fu: AFUN is an affordance foundation model that predicts task-conditional functional masks and 3D post-contact motion from RGB-D observations and language task descriptions. The authors built a large-scale standardized data pipeline converting heterogeneous robot, human, simulation, and scan data into a shared affordance schema. The model achieves substantial improvements over baselines on affordance segmentation, contact-point prediction, and 3D motion across multiple benchmarks and generalizes to

item.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
item.description: Priyaranjan Pattnayak: Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER) is a training-free evaluation metric that transliterates reference and hypothesis text into a canonical script before computing Word Error Rate. It addresses the problem of WER overestimating errors in multilingual ASR where romanized and native-script outputs encode the same words differently, reducing inflated model gaps by up to 12% on Indic language datasets.

item.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
item.description: Liuliu Chen, Gowri Rajaram, Eleanor Bailey: Researchers developed a three-stage machine learning approach that augments traditional ML with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm from Emergency Department triage notes. The system achieved high AUPRC scores across internal and external validation at three Australian hospitals without site-specific retraining, and identifies primary self-harm methods with 95% accuracy for granular surveillance.

item.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: Junxia Cui, Haotian Ye, Runchu Tian: SimSD applies speculative decoding to diffusion language models by introducing a plug-and-play masking strategy that preserves temporally valid token-level contexts. The method allows draft tokens from a draft model to be verified in a single forward pass, achieving up to 7.46x throughput improvement while maintaining generation quality.

item.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
item.description: Yuting Ning, Zhehao Zhang, Yash Kumar Lal: SkillHarm is a benchmark and automated construction pipeline for evaluating skill-based attacks across the agent skill-use lifecycle. It defines two attack scenarios (Fixed-Payload Poisoning and Self-Mutating Poisoning), introduces a taxonomy of 12 risk types targeting data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy, and demonstrates high attack success rates up to 86.3% on current agents.

item.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
item.description: Jonah Leshin, Manish Shah, Ian Timmis: The paper introduces a technique for tracking behavioral changes in adapting agents by embedding skill file diffs and learning trait vectors as directions in embedding space. A linear model trained on labeled before-after pairs achieves 91.2% sign classification accuracy on the propensity-to-seek-sensitive-data trait and is integrated into an agent-to-agent evaluation protocol.

item.title: LL-Bench: Rethinking Low-Level Vision Evaluation in the Era of Large-Scale Generative Models
item.description: Lu Liu, Huiyu Duan, Chenxin Zhu: LL-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark comprising 2,469 real-world degraded images across 16 low-level vision tasks, with 28,919 restored images from 31 models annotated with expert pairwise preferences and quality scores. The paper reveals performance boundaries of large-scale generative models on pixel-wise vision tasks and proposes LL-Score, an MLLM-based quality evaluator that aligns better with human preferences than existing metrics.

item.title: Build a Basic AI Agent from Scratch: Tools
item.description: ruxudev: A guide on implementing AI agents from first principles by building tools. The article covers foundational techniques for constructing basic agents, with emphasis on the tooling layer that enables agent functionality.

item.title: Improving Combined Detection and Classification of TEM Defects via Mask-Conditioned Latent Diffusion Augmentation
item.description: Ni Li, Nuohao Liu, Ryan Jacobs: The paper introduces mask-conditioned latent diffusion model augmentation to synthesize realistic TEM images with labeled multi-class defect masks, addressing data scarcity in microscopy. Synthetic data augmentation trains Mask R-CNN for joint defect detection and classification, yielding modest F1 score improvements of up to 0.02 on small experimental datasets.

item.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
item.description: Hao Li, Jingkun An, Zijun Song: SafeSteer is a method that performs on-policy distillation confined to safety tokens to align LLMs with human values while minimizing capability degradation. The approach constructs a safety teacher via activation steering and uses a safety token selection algorithm, restricting KL penalties only to identified safety tokens during training.

item.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
item.description: Wenbin Wu: Researchers develop an audit framework to detect systematic biases toward specific financial assets in large language models. They apply behavioral auditing, sparse autoencoder feature analysis, and downstream impact testing to show that Bitcoin preferences in models like Gemma 3 are tied to identifiable internal representations and affect portfolio allocation decisions.

item.title: Why Not Hyperparameter-Friendly Optimisation? A Monotonic Adaptive Norm Rescaling Approach For Long-Tailed Recognition
item.description: Shuo Zhang, Chenqi Li, Tingting Zhu: Self-Adaptive Monotonic Normalization (SAMN) is a hyperparameter-free approach for long-tailed recognition that enforces monotonicity on per-class weight norms using the Pool Adjacent Violators Algorithm during the classifier retraining stage. The method avoids parameter regularization while achieving state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets.

item.title: FigSIM: A Dataset for Fine-grained Suicide Severity and Figurative Language in Suicide Memes
item.description: Liuliu Chen, Elise R. Carrotte, Brian E. Chapman: FigSIM is a dataset of 1049 memes annotated for suicide severity levels, figurative language phenomena, and suicide-related content. The dataset benchmarks 16 unimodal and multimodal models on figurative language, severity, and content detection tasks, revealing model biases in handling figurative memes.

item.title: Moment-Video: Diagnosing Temporal Fidelity of Video MLLMs on Momentary Visual Events
item.description: Xiaolin Liu, Yilun Zhu, Xiangyu Zhao: Moment-Video is a benchmark with 1,000 video-QA pairs designed to diagnose temporal fidelity in video MLLMs by testing their ability to understand momentary visual events lasting only a few frames. The benchmark covers seven domains and four task types: Temporal Occurrence, Temporal Counting, Action Description, and Temporal Reasoning. Evaluation of 33 video MLLMs shows significant performance gaps, with the best model achieving only 39.6% accuracy and most open-source models below 25%.
